steps.title: Identify the exponential pattern
steps.content: Each increase of $1$ in $x$ multiplies $y$ by $4$, so the relationship is exponential with base $4$.

steps.title: Use the initial value
steps.content: When $x=0$, $y=150$. In an exponential equation $y=a(4)^x$, the value at $x=0$ is $a(4)^0=a$, so $a=150$.

steps.title: Write and confirm the equation
steps.content: The equation is $$y=150(4)^x.$$ Two other options are power functions with $x$ as the base. $y = 4(150)^x$ has initial value $4$. $y = 150(4)^x$ matches both the initial value and the multiplier, so B is correct.
